senses.definition: each of the twelve named divisions of the year, like January, February, etc.

examples.x: I like to set goals for myself at the start of each month.
My father pays his bills at the beginning of the month.
My favorite month is December because of the holidays.
The school semester starts next month.

senses.definition: the first month of the year, after December and before February

examples.x: In January, we celebrate the start of the new year with fireworks and joy.
January is the first month of the year and marks the beginning of the winter.
The winter weather in January brings frosty mornings and cozy evenings by the fireplace, perfect for hot cocoa and warm blankets.
As January progresses, daylight hours gradually increase, signaling the slow transition from winter to spring.
Many retailers offer post-holiday sales in January, making it an ideal time to snag deals on winter clothing and seasonal items.

senses.definition: the second month of the year, after January and before March

examples.x: During February, the days gradually start getting longer as we move closer to spring.
February is the second month of the year and follows January.
In some countries, February is known as the month of love and romance.
In February, the air begins to feel a bit milder as winter gradually gives way to the promise of spring.
As February comes to a close, thoughts turn to the anticipation of longer days and the arrival of spring, bringing hope and renewal after the winter months.

senses.definition: the third month of the year, after February and before April
the fourth month of the year, after March and before May

examples.x: Earth Day is observed on April 22nd to promote environmental awareness.
The weather in April can be unpredictable, with both rain and sunshine.
April showers bring May flowers, a saying that reflects the unpredictable weather patterns of this transitional month.
In many cultures, April is associated with Easter, a Christian holiday celebrating the resurrection of Jesus Christ, marked by traditions like egg hunts and religious services.
Tax Day in the United States typically falls on April 15th, the deadline for individuals to file their income tax returns for the previous year.

senses.definition: the fifth month of the year, after April and before June
the sixth month of the year, after May and before July

examples.x: June is a month filled with outdoor activities like swimming, camping, and barbecues.
Many students look forward to June because it marks the beginning of summer vacation.
June marks the official start of summer in the Northern Hemisphere, bringing longer days, warmer temperatures, and outdoor activities like beach outings and barbecues.
Father's Day, celebrated on the third Sunday of June in various countries, honors fathers and fatherhood with gifts, cards, and family gatherings.
Graduation ceremonies are commonly held in June, recognizing the achievements of students completing their studies at various levels, from high school to university.

senses.definition: the seventh month of the year, after June and before August

examples.x: Fireworks displays are common in many countries during the month of July.
July is a peak month for summer vacations, with families traveling to beaches, resorts, national parks, and other destinations for relaxation and adventure.
In some regions, July marks the beginning of harvest season, with farmers busy harvesting crops such as corn, tomatoes, berries, and other fruits and vegetables.
Various festivals and events take place in July around the world, celebrating culture, music, food, and traditions, attracting locals and tourists alike to participate in the festivities.

senses.definition: the eighth month of the year, after July and before September
the ninth month of the year, after August and before October

examples.x: In September, the weather starts to cool down as autumn approaches.
September is a good time to visit the beach and enjoy the last days of summer.
Many students return to school in September, and parents and teachers prepare for the academic year ahead, organizing classrooms, purchasing textbooks, and planning curriculum.
September often sees the start of various cultural events and festivals, including harvest festivals, art exhibitions, and music concerts, offering entertainment options for communities.
September can be a busy month for businesses as they gear up for the holiday season, with retailers stocking shelves with fall merchandise and planning promotions to attract customers.

senses.definition: the tenth month of the year, after September and before November

examples.x: Halloween is celebrated on the last day of October.
Many people look forward to October because it marks the beginning of the holiday season.
October is known for its vibrant autumn foliage, as trees across many regions turn shades of red, orange, and yellow, creating picturesque landscapes.

In some cultures, October is associated with harvest festivals and agricultural celebrations, where communities come together to give thanks for the bounty of the season and share in traditional foods and rituals.
Many people enjoy cozying up with warm beverages like apple cider or hot chocolate in October, as they embrace the transition to fall and prepare for the upcoming holiday season.

senses.definition: the 11th month of the year, after October and before December

examples.x: Many people enjoy watching the colorful leaves fall from the trees in November.
November is the month when many people celebrate Thanksgiving.
As temperatures drop, November marks the beginning of the holiday season, with many people starting to decorate their homes and prepare for festive celebrations.

In some regions, November brings the first snowfall of the season, transforming landscapes into winter wonderlands and offering opportunities for outdoor activities like skiing, snowboarding, and building snowmen.

November is also known for events such as Veterans Day, Remembrance Day, and Black Friday, which commemorate veterans, honor the memory of fallen soldiers, and kick off the holiday shopping season, respectively.

senses.definition: the 12th and last month of the year, after November and before January

examples.x: December is the last month of the year.
Many people decorate their homes with lights and ornaments in December for the holiday season.
Some people set resolutions for the new year in December, hoping to make positive changes.
December is a month of festive celebrations, with many cultures around the world observing holidays such as Christmas, Hanukkah, and Kwanzaa with decorations, gatherings, and gift-giving.
In some countries, December 31st is celebrated as New Year's Eve, a night of festivities, fireworks, and countdowns to welcome the start of a fresh year with hope and optimism.

senses.definition: a period of time that a year is divided into, such as winter and summer, with each having three months

examples.x: During the spring season, my son enjoys swimming in the pool.
Summer is my favorite season because I can go to the beach.
The season of fall is a beautiful time to take nature walks and see colorful leaves.
The season of summer brings warm weather and longer days.
Winter is the perfect season to build snowmen and have snowball fights.

senses.definition: the season that comes after winter, when in most countries the trees and flowers begin to grow again

examples.x: His favorite season is spring, when the weather is mild and the flowers are in full bloom.
Many people do spring cleaning to tidy up their homes.
The birds start building their nests in the spring.
The days become longer in spring, and we have more daylight.
The spring semester at school starts in January and ends in May, with a break for spring break in March.

senses.definition: the season that comes after spring and in most countries summer is the warmest season

examples.x: I enjoy eating ice cream to cool down in the hot summer months.
I like to have a picnic in the park and enjoy the beautiful weather in summer.
I love going for bike rides and feeling the warm summer breeze on my face.
Many fruits, such as watermelon and berries, are in season during the summer.
Summer is the season for outdoor concerts and festivals.

senses.definition: the season that comes after summer, when in most countries the color of the leaves change and they fall from the trees

examples.x: Fall is the season when students go back to school after summer break.
I like to wear cozy sweaters and scarves during the fall season.
In fall, you can see squirrels gathering nuts to store for the winter.
The days become shorter in fall, and the nights become cooler.
The sound of crunching leaves underfoot is a characteristic of the fall season.

senses.definition: the season that comes after fall and in most countries winter is the coldest season

examples.x: It's fun to go skiing or snowboarding on the mountains in winter.
My children look forward to receiving gifts during the winter holidays.
Winter brings a peaceful silence, especially after a fresh snowfall.
Winter is a time when birds fly south to escape the cold weather.
Winter is the time when people celebrate holidays like Christmas and New Year's.
